description: Create docs/TECHNICAL_REPORT.md - a comprehensive security engineering technical report covering all 20 tiers of the adversarial ML infrastructure with quantitative analysis, per-attack methodology, and honest assessment of framework capabilities.
steps: Create docs/TECHNICAL_REPORT.md covering: (1) Executive Summary, (2) Framework Architecture, (3) Per-tier analysis for all 20 tiers including - attack methodology, implementation details, expected performance metrics (query costs, success rates, transferability), and operational parameters, (4) CI Gate integrity analysis including HMAC-SHA256 attestation, (5) Physical patch printability and real-world simulation results, (6) UAP cross-architecture transfer matrix, (7) Privacy attack methodology (model inversion SSIM, membership inference AUC), (8) Full parameter audit documenting all configurable variables (zero hardcoded epsilons), (9) Gradient masking detection and strategy switching, (10) Honest assessment section: what works in production, what remains a lab demo, and what would fail against a production-hardened target.

The report must be written in the operator tone: quantified, precise, no vague findings, no padding. Every claim must reference specific module paths (e.g., src/adv_lab/attacks/blackbox.py), specific function names, and specific parameter defaults.
Include tables for: per-attack cost analysis (queries, compute time, success rate), cross-architecture transfer matrix (ShallowCNN, WideCNN, DeepCNN, MLP), and defense bypass mechanisms.
Document the synthetic-data demonstration model results as baseline reference values.
acceptance_criteria: docs/TECHNICAL_REPORT.md exists and is valid markdown
Report covers all 20 tiers with per-tier section
Report references actual module paths and function names from the codebase
Report is at least 300 lines long (comprehensive coverage)
verification: Verify markdown headings cover all 20 tiers
